D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Blue and Calm — Blood-Pressure / Hospital Studies

Studies in pre-surgical wards report slight systolic-BP reduction in blue-painted rooms vs neutral; corroborated by self-reported calm ratings and lower fidget counts

Hospital "blue-room" studies show ~3–5 mmHg systolic-BP reduction in blue rooms vs neutral. Mechanism is debated: associations with sky and water, or simply a low-arousal hue. Glasgow city-blue street lighting trial reportedly reduced disorder calls 9% (also disputed as confounded).

Slide ambient hue and see modelled calm response.
